name: Bernard Purdie
description: American R&B, soul and funk drummer
abstract: Bernard Lee "Pretty" Purdie (born June 11, 1939) is an American drummer, and an influential R&B, soul, funk and jazz musician. He is known for his precise time-keeping and his signature use of triplets against a half-time backbeat: the Purdie shuffle.

interviews.title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: David Spinozza
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was Chuck Rainey, Bernard Purdie, Richard T, and me. And that’s the first time I heard those guys play.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I did a lot with them... We did a lot of things, Bernard Purdie and Richard Tee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth & Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: So we used him on a record date, John and I. He played “Sing Sing Sing”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth & Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Saddle River, New Jersey, January 19, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: Purdie. Yes. Yeah Purdie’s a master man. You know it’s as simple as that. He’s strong, he’s determined, he knows music and he knows what he’s looking for in his sound and he knows how to make it work.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: Like the drummer for Aretha Franklin, Bernard Purdie — ... So when we’d go into a studio Bernard Purdie would set up all these music stands... He was a really fine drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: At the time I was studying too, with Bernard Purdie... And he also helped me put together how to secure a publishing company... given them one tune and learn the ropes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
